dump

dump file period tag tag tag...

This command causes the simulation data represented by the tags to be dumped to file every period of simulation time. The data are all dumped in standard tab-separated format, with a header giving the tags, such as might be accepted by a spreadsheet. The simulation time is automatically printed as the first column of output.

If `-' is given as the file name, the data is dumped to the terminal via standard output. If `+' is given as the filename, then data is dumped to the terminal via standard error. It is more usual to dump output to the terminal with dump-stats.

Usually, the tags are names of species or tags created with plex-dump or omniplex-dump. Some built-in tags also exist:

  1. sim-time - The simulation time.

  2. clock-time - Rough clock time since simulation started. Unfortunately, this is very limited in magnitude, and it will eventually "roll over" and become a negative number.

  3. volume - Current simulation volume in liters.

  4. reaction-count - Number of reactions executed since start of simulation.

  5. activation-count - Number of reactions rescheduled since start of simulation. When a reaction occurs, it changes the populations of the substrates of other reactions, whose next probable time of occurrance will therefore change. Rescheduling these reactions uses more clock time than any other activity, and in fact tends to proceed at a relatively constant rate with respect to clock time. Reactions may cause more or less of this rescheduling, so the rate of reaction with respect to clock time varies considerably.
